issues.title: Initiative Table & Persistence
issues.body_preview:
A persistent table for Initiatives, separate from the Job table. Initiatives own *why* and *when*; Jobs own *what* and *how*. When an Initiative's cron fires, it creates (or re-runs) a Job — the Initiative itself doesn't execute work directly.

## Why
The Job Graph provides execution. But recurring,

issues.title: User Statement Log
issues.body_preview:
A persistent, append-only log of user statements the system considers *evidence* for work. Provides an N:N join between Jobs and Statements, enabling provenance queries, drift detection, and conflict detection across Initiatives.

## Why
Jobs and Initiatives need traceable justification. When the sy

issues.title: Developer/Writer Branch-Based Script Staging
issues.body_preview:
Replace the flat `runtime/staging/scripts/` directory with a git branch workflow: each DW session creates a feature branch (keyed by GUID), scripts are committed there, and promotion to live means merging to main and pulling into `src/instance/scripts/`.

## Motivation
The current staging model (wri

issues.title: Web Dashboard
issues.body_preview:
A browser-based UI served from within the AgentOS container. Provides human visibility into the running system and a surface for resolving FAFC confirmations, without requiring terminal access.

## Motivation
As the system grows more autonomous, the human needs low-friction ways to inspect what it's

issues.title: Script Branch Workflow + Code-Reviewing-Agent
issues.body_preview:
Scripts are developed on branches and merged to main only after a PR review. A Code-Reviewing-Agent participates in the review before merge, providing quality and correctness assessment. Formalizes script promotion with an accountable audit trail accessible via terminal and Gitea web UI.

## Motivat

issues.title: Gitea Integration
issues.body_preview:
Gitea embedded in the AgentOS container, hosting the script repository. A port is exposed so the human operator can inspect scripts, history, and PRs via browser without SSH access. Agents interact with Gitea via its API for branch and PR operations.

## Motivation
Scripts are the system's growing c

issues.title: Multi-source command input: array of input sources in config
issues.body_preview:
## Context

Currently the Gist command channel is configured via a single `WRIT_COMMAND_GIST_ID` in `.env`. This is sufficient for a single-user, single-channel setup.

## Future direction

Support an array of input sources so you can:
- Add channels (e.g., a second Gist for a different user)
- Revo

issues.title: ClawdBot Integration & Untrusted Execution
issues.body_preview:
A potential sandbox model for imported ClawdBot skills and agents that lack Writ-native review guarantees. Also covers a vetting pipeline to transform imported skills into proper Writ scripts or agents.

## Motivation
ClawdBot and similar systems may offer useful capabilities that would be expensive

issues.title: Support per-agent model selection for Ollama provider
issues.body_preview:
## Summary

Currently `OLLAMA_MODEL` is a single env var used for all agents. Different agents have different strengths — the orchestrator needs strong reasoning/dialogue, while the planner/executor/developer-writer need structured output and code generation.

## Proposed approach

Add per-agent mod

issues.title: Scenario CI via Claude Code Web
issues.body_preview:
Use Claude Code web sessions as an automated integration testing environment. Each session boots a clean Writ instance, feeds it behavioral scenarios, observes results, and validates that the system performs as expected — including script authoring, review, and execution.

## Motivation
Unit tests v

issues.title: BIG_BROTHER Proactive Config Optimization
issues.body_preview:
A proactive mode for BIG_BROTHER that analyzes accumulated execution logs and proposes agent config improvements based on observed patterns — not just in response to RR flags.

## Motivation
The reactive path (RR flag → BB fix) addresses individual violations. A proactive path would detect systemic

issues.title: Script intake pipeline for third-party scripts
issues.body_preview:
When Writ ingests a script from an external source (downloaded, user-provided, community library), it should pass through a review pipeline before promotion to `src/instance/scripts/`.

**Proposed flow:**
1. **Download/receive** script into a quarantine directory (e.g., `runtime/staging/intake/`)
2.

issues.title: Concurrent execution guard: lockfile or queue for Gist poll + pipeline overlap
issues.body_preview:
## Context

Cron fires on a fixed schedule. If a previous command is still being processed when the next poll runs, there is no guard preventing overlap.

## Problem

- Cron fires → poll script writes `A.cmd` to inbox
- Pipeline starts processing `A.cmd`
- Cron fires again → poll script writes `B.cm

issues.title: Self-scheduled polling: replace bootstrap cron with Writ-managed job
issues.body_preview:
## Context

The Gist command channel currently relies on a bootstrap cron job to poll for incoming commands (see `docs/deployment/gist-command-channel.md`). This is a deliberate temporary measure.

## What needs to happen

Writ should eventually schedule its own polling via its internal job/initiati

issues.title: Script discovery for agents: registry or convention for available scripts
issues.body_preview:
## Context

Agents need a way to know what scripts are available and what they do. Currently there is no registry or discovery convention for this.

## Problem

When an agent (e.g., Lieutenant Planner) needs to delegate work to a shell script, it has no reliable way to enumerate available scripts, u

issues.title: IOAdapter inbox integration: read .cmd files from runtime/inbox/ into pipeline
issues.body_preview:
## Context

The Gist command channel (see `docs/deployment/gist-command-channel.md`) writes plaintext commands as `.cmd` files to `runtime/inbox/`. This issue tracks wiring that inbox into the Writ pipeline.

## What needs to happen

- `IOAdapter` (or a new `GistAdapter`) should watch/read `.cmd` fi

issues.title: Containerization
issues.body_preview:
AgentOS ships as a self-contained deployable container. All internal services — Gitea, script runner, logs, dashboard — run inside it. A user deploys one container and gets a fully functional instance.

## Motivation
The 'agent as a self-expanding OS' analogy implies a deployable unit. Containerizat

issues.title: Throbber / Acknowledgment UX
issues.body_preview:
Per-channel activity indicators and acknowledgment patterns for asynchronous job execution. Each IOAdapter implementation handles its own throbber/spinner; the Orchestrator provides the timing contract.

## Motivation
When a job takes longer than expected, the user needs feedback: either a visual in

issues.title: System health assessment: proactive agent config coherence
issues.body_preview:
## What
A periodic assessment that checks all agent configs (XYZ-AGENT.md files) for coherence against CONSTITUTION.md and SOUL.md, outside of RR flag triggers. When drift is detected — configs that contradict core values, are internally inconsistent, or reference deprecated patterns — the system fl

issues.title: Model management: route requests to cheapest competent model per task
issues.body_preview:
## What
A model router that selects the cheapest capable model for each agent invocation based on task type. Orchestrator/Reviewer tasks need strong reasoning; Planner/Executor/DW tasks need structured JSON output. The router maps agent role → appropriate model tier, with per-agent overrides availab

issues.title: Prompt evolution: A/B testing of agent prompts
issues.body_preview:
## What
A framework for A/B testing changes to agent prompts (XYZ-AGENT.md files) with tracked performance metrics. BIG_BROTHER proposes a prompt change → the system runs both variants for N requests → metrics are compared → the winning variant is promoted. All of this goes through the existing revi

issues.title: Get-to-know-the-user: proactive onboarding and USER.md
issues.body_preview:
## What
A proactive onboarding loop that builds a persistent `USER.md` file capturing what the system has learned about the user: their preferences, working style, recurring goals, and known system shortcomings relevant to them. Writ periodically updates USER.md through conversation and discloses kn

issues.title: Adjutant: cron-based maintenance agent
issues.body_preview:
## What
The Adjutant is the system's maintenance and self-management agent. It runs on a cron schedule (via the Initiative system), performs periodic health checks, coordinates housekeeping tasks (log rotation, job store pruning, config coherence), and can act as a gatekeeper for tasks that require

issues.title: Gist-based command channel
issues.body_preview:
## What
A pull-based command channel using a private GitHub Gist as a dead drop. A cron-driven poll script fetches the Gist, writes commands as `.cmd` files to `runtime/inbox/`, and a post script writes responses back. The IOAdapter integration reads inbox files and feeds them into `handleRequest()`

issues.title: Initiative system: InitiativeBuilder agent
issues.body_preview:
## What
An InitiativeBuilder agent that receives a user request identified by the Orchestrator as initiative-worthy (recurring pattern, time-bounded goal) and creates a persisted Initiative entry — including cron expression, architecture type, stop condition, and evidence links back to user statemen
